description: §65.6 — the `<theme>` is the single OWNER of the variant set. It declares base + `.Dark`, so the variant type of `@mode` is `{ Light, Dark }`. Reassigning `@mode = .Nonexistent` (a variant NOT in the theme) is checked against the theme's variant set and fires E-TYPE-063 (error). This proves the theme's variant set is enforced downstream — you cannot switch to a variant the theme does not define.
rationale: §65.6: the theme owns the variant set (no separate enum, no duplication). The inferred variant type of `@mode` includes only the base (`Light`, from the initial `.Light`) + the declared `.Dark`. A reassign to `.Nonexistent` is a non-member of that set → E-TYPE-063 (variant not in the type). The cell IS typed (by the theme), so this is a type error, NOT E-VARIANT-AMBIGUOUS.
